meta.title: Indian Grandparents in Toronto - Community & Resources
meta.description: Society of Indian Grandparents (SIG) - Connecting elderly Indians in Greater Toronto. Find resources, events, and community support for Indian seniors.

nav.ariaTheme: Change color theme - tap to switch between light, dark, and high contrast modes
nav.ariaLang: Switch language between English and Hindi

hero.heading: Welcome to Your Community
hero.description: We are the Society of Indian Grandparents. We are a community to connect elderly Indians in the Greater Toronto region with each other - find resources, make friends, and share in activities that were common to experience in India but are hard to find in the West.

community.heading: Our Community
community.description: Connect with fellow Indian grandparents in the Greater Toronto Area. Share experiences, make friends, and build lasting relationships.

about.heading: About Us
about.description: The Society of Indian Grandparents (SIG) is dedicated to combating loneliness among Indian seniors in the West by building a culturally resonant peer community.

q1.question: How do I get a senior's TTC card?
q1.answer: Seniors 65+ can apply for a TTC Senior card online or at any Shoppers Drug Mart. Bring ID with your birthdate.

q2.question: Where can I find Indian groceries?
q2.answer: Popular areas include Gerrard India Bazaar, Brampton, and Mississauga. Stores like Patel Brothers and Oceans are well-stocked.

q3.question: How do I find a Hindi-speaking doctor?
q3.answer: Contact Health Care Connect at 1-800-445-1822 and request a Hindi-speaking physician. Many clinics in Brampton and Scarborough have Hindi-speaking staff.
